top_signals.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
top_signals.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The risk of taking over a vessel without its history
top_signals.summary: Why maintenance records matter at handover Marine insurers have long emphasised that maintenance records should follow the vessel at changes of ownership or management. top_signals.title: EmissionLink warns cheap FuelEU compliance could weaken fuel transition
top_signals.summary: EmissionLink has warned that falling FuelEU Maritime surplus prices could make compliance cheaper for shipowners, but weaken the regulation’s ability to drive real uptake of lower-carbon fuels. As the first FuelEU reporting year closed at the end of June, the company said the regulation had succeeded in creating an active compliance market, with shipowners moving ...

top_signals.title: ENGINE: Europe and Africa Fuel Availability Outlook
top_signals.summary: Northwest Europe Bunker fuel availability is tight in the ARA for prompt supplies, with buyers advised to book stems around 5-7 days in advance to get delivery of any fuel grade, a trader said. The ARA’s independently held fuel oil stocks have increased 6% higher on average so far in July, compared to June’s monthly ...

top_signals.title: Bitcoin may have bottomed before its traditional cycle low: Grayscale’s Pandl
top_signals.summary: Grayscale’s head of research said that Bitcoin may have bottomed, arguing that macro factors, such as interest rate decisions as increasingly driving the price action of this maturing asset class.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Circle partners with Kakao, Toss Bank to explore stablecoin payment rails in South Korea
top_signals.summary: Circle plans to explore blockchain-based payment infrastructure in South Korea through partnerships with Kakao and Toss.

top_signals.title: Project CAPTURED advances regulatory pathways for onboard captured CO2
top_signals.summary: Project CAPTURED has achieved two regulatory milestones that strengthen the commercial case for onboard carbon capture and storage (OCCS), following its world’s first demonstration of an end-to-end value chain for onboard captured and liquefied CO2 (LCO2). Completed in June 2025, the pilot showed that CO2 captured onboard a vessel can be offloaded ship-to-ship, transported overland ...

top_signals.title: Ship Recycling Market Finding Renewed Support
top_signals.summary: The ship recycling market gained ground last week, as supply remained short. In its latest weekly report, Best Oasis (www.best-oasis.com), a leading cash buyer of ships, said that “the Indian market strengthened during the week due to the limited availability of steel plates, as slow cutting activity and several vessels remaining away from shore restricted ...

top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Rises to 1-Week High
top_signals.summary: The Baltic dry index rose 1.7% to 2,715 on Wednesday, recording its first gain since July 14. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes, including iron ore and coal, advanced 4.7% to 4,114, its highest level since July 16. On the other hand, the panamax index, which usually carries 60,000 to 70,000 tons of ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Weekly Dry Market Monitor: Port Hedland Average Waiting Time – Iron-Ore Congestion Meets Labour Action
top_signals.summary: Waiting time reached multi-month highs. The average vessel waiting time at Port Hedland rose to approximately 8.7 days in the week to 17 July, up from about 8.4 days a week earlier. The 30-day moving average stood at 8.66 days, close to the highest level recorded over the past 18 months, highlighting persistently elevated congestion ...
